articles.title: How a Pop Star Helps Opera Grow
articles.summary: Opera is a type of music and theater that some people thought was old-fashioned, but now it's becoming more popular again. The Welsh National Opera says opera is more relevant today because of pop star Rosalía. She makes music that mixes opera with pop, and her songs are used in TikTok videos. This has brought new fans to opera. Also, people on social media like 'opera aesthetics,' which means dramatic and fancy styles. The WNO started 80 years ago with amateur singers like miners and teachers. They performed in a theater that is now a pub. Even though opera is getting more support, the WNO has faced money problems and had to cut back on tours. This shows how old art forms can change and stay exciting for new audiences.

articles.title: TV Stars Fix Up an Old Chapel in Wales
articles.summary: Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth are TV stars who are fixing up an old chapel in north Wales to make it their dream home. They bought the chapel for £200,000 in 2022 and moved in over a year ago. The chapel is 163 years old and was empty for 14 years before they bought it. They are working hard on renovations and sharing their journey on a TV show called Our Welsh Chapel Dream. People in the town of Pwllheli are friendly and often stop by to say hello or offer them drinks. Keith and Marj feel happy in their new community and want to respect the chapel's history, which includes weddings and funerals. They are excited to turn this old building into a cozy home.

articles.title: Oil Prices Drop as Iran Opens a Key Waterway
articles.summary: Oil prices went down a lot after Iran said it would keep the Strait of Hormuz open for ships during a ceasefire. The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway where much of the world's oil travels. When it was closed, oil prices went up because less oil could get through. Now that Iran says it's open, prices fell from over $98 to $88 per barrel. This is good news for people who buy gas because lower oil prices can mean cheaper fuel. The U.S. president is happy about this, but some shipping groups are still checking to make sure it's safe. Markets around the world went up because of this news. The Strait had been closed since February when there were military strikes, causing oil to become very expensive. Even though Iran says it's open, some experts warn ships to be careful because there might still be dangers like mines.

articles.title: Many Young Men Live with Parents to Save Money
articles.summary: In the UK, more than one in three young men aged 20-35 live with their parents in 2025, up from 26% in 2000. This is because renting and buying houses are very expensive. Young women also live with parents more, but not as much as men. Nathan, a 24-year-old, lives with his dad and saves money by not paying high rent. He works cleaning trains and has saved £50,000. He cooks his own food and spends little on fun things. Harry, 22, moved back with his mum to finish school because everything costs too much. Living with parents helps young people save money for the future.

articles.title: Tennis Player Cameron Norrie Loses a Big Match
articles.summary: Cameron Norrie, a tennis player from Britain, lost a match at the Barcelona Open tournament. He was beaten by Rafael Jodar, a young player from Spain, in two sets with scores of 6-3 and 6-2. The match lasted 69 minutes. Jodar played very well on the clay court. Norrie is ranked 24th in the world, but Jodar is only 19 years old and was ranked much lower last year. Jodar said he was happy with his win and will keep trying hard. In other tennis news, Katie Boulter and Coco Gauff also lost their matches in different tournaments. This shows that young players can surprise everyone in sports.

articles.title: Tennis Star's Stress and a Doping Test Problem
articles.summary: Marketa Vondrousova is a tennis player who won Wimbledon in 2023. In December 2025, she refused to let a doping control officer into her home for a test. She says she was very stressed and scared because the officer came late at night without following proper rules. The International Tennis Integrity Agency (ITIA) has charged her with refusing the test, which could lead to a ban of up to four years if she is found guilty. Vondrousova explains that she was not trying to avoid the test but felt unsafe. She has been dealing with anxiety and stress, and experts have confirmed her condition. She can still play tennis while the investigation continues, but she has not played recently due to a shoulder injury. This story shows how mental health can affect athletes in sports.
